findingDetails.title: Soft-deleted memory rows remain active to list/retrieval/dedup surfaces
findingDetails.description: When SPECKIT_SOFT_DELETE_TOMBSTONES is enabled, delete only stamps deleted_at. Active reads/listing and same-path dedup do not filter deleted_at or remove active_memory_projection, so a deleted row can stay visible and can make a later identical save return unchanged instead of creating a live replacement.
findingDetails.impact: memory_delete can report success while the deleted record remains addressable/search-visible and blocks re-save until a separate purge path runs.
findingDetails.recommendation: On soft delete, remove the row from active_memory_projection and require active read/dedup paths to add deleted_at IS NULL; ensure re-save ignores tombstoned predecessors unless explicitly purging/recovering.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Scope spec targets memory store/index/lifecycle code under mcp_server and write-path safety.
